answer:
The convolution integral \( y(\tau) = \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} x(\tau - t) v(t) \, dt \) at \(\tau = 2.5\) computes the overlap area of \(x(t)\) and \(v(t)\) when one is shifted.  
Here, \(v(t)\) is nonzero for \(0 \le t \le 2\), and \(x(2.5 - t)\) is nonzero when \(0 \le 2.5 - t \le 3\), i.e., \(-0.5 \le t \le 2.5\).  
The overlap interval is \(0 \le t \le 2\), giving an area of \(2 \times 1 = 2\).  

\boxed{C}
